summary: Flip evidence is internally consistent and scope-preserving, with no second production flip found. Release still needs triage for an incomplete telemetry backstop, broken local/CI parity, and a prohibited single-consumer seam.

findings.title: Capacity telemetry can fail while the backstop stays green
findings.body: The absent-guard rationale assumes FilesBackupStale covers this sensor pipeline. However, scripts/backup-files-data.sh:133-149 substitutes 0 for empty df results while publishing a fresh backup-success timestamp. Because push_metrics is called under `||`, Bash does not abort its body on those failures. This can produce 0/0 or value/0: FilesBulkSSDLow remains silent or false-pages while FilesBackupStale stays healthy. BulkStorageLow mitigates total disk coverage, but the claimed independent host sensor is not fail-loud.
findings.recommendation: Narrow the comment to total launchd/push failure and add a follow-up to validate both df values before publishing the success heartbeat, or introduce a separate capacity-telemetry heartbeat. Adding absent() alone will not detect zero-valued corrupt samples.

findings.title: The new required gate is missing from the documented local CI path
findings.body: The workflow adds the bulk-SSD replay to the sole required `gate`, but `make ci` still invokes only the alertmanager containerized e2e. This violates CONTRIBUTING.md:56-62 and Makefile:98's promise that `make ci` reproduces the required gate, allowing the documented local pre-push check to pass without exercising this regression.
findings.recommendation: Restore parity through a shared CI driver or invoke this replay from `make ci` when Docker is available, and strengthen test_make-ci-parity.bats to assert the bulk-SSD gate is present on both paths.

findings.title: New shared replay seam has only one consumer
findings.body: This file declares common vmalert replay primitives while explicitly retaining the existing drift harness as an inline copy. Repository-wide references show only vmalert-bulkssd-firing-e2e.sh consumes the library, and the Review Decision Log contains no exception. That violates the release rule requiring at least two real adapters or a Decision-Log justification and leaves two supposedly related harness implementations free to drift.
findings.recommendation: For this frozen RED/GREEN branch, record a human-approved Decision-Log justification and a separate migration/refactor follow-up. Otherwise, remove the premature abstraction or migrate a second consumer with freshly generated machine-owned evidence.

next_steps: Triage the three findings before merge; the first should become an explicit follow-up rather than a second alert flip in this bugfix.
If any harness or command changes, regenerate both script-owned verify records and verification evidence.
Preserve the current frozen fixture and symptom-token-bearing RED tail.
